equivalence_argument: The port creates an SPC verifier for the async RPC module, but Ethereum node component construction does not inject that verifier into an executor provider.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Ethereum node component construction does not create and inject a verifier-backed executor provider.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ethereum/node/src/node.rs creates SpcVerifier::new_from_env_simple only for ritual_async_rpc::module.
crates/ethereum/node/src/node.rs build_evm returns EthEvmConfig::new(ctx.chain_spec()) and does not construct an executor provider with SPC verification.
ritual-reth-nodebuilder-internal expects ctx.spc_verifier(), but the audited port BuilderContext has no such method.

rationale: The primary diff imports the concrete SPC verifier, creates VerificationConfig and ExecutorRegistry, wraps it in SpcVerifierImpl, and replaces BasicBlockExecutorProvider::new with new_with_spc(Some(spc_verifier)). No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When building Ethereum node components, the executor provider must be constructed with an SPC verifier created from default verification config and an in-memory executor registry, rather than using a BasicBlockExecutorProvider without SPC verification.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instantiate EthereumNode components and assert the returned executor provider contains an SPC verifier; simulate ConcreteSpcVerifier::new failure and assert components() returns an error instead of silently constructing an executor without SPC verification.
summary: The node builds an RPC verifier but does not inject one into block execution.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Instantiate Ethereum node components and assert the executor provider contains an SPC verifier; the current port only wires a verifier into the RPC module.
title: Node builder injects SPC verifier
